Foundations — Colors

The codified Precision Blue color system, locked 2026-07-21 after eight art-direction rounds (full trail: context/design-language.html). Two hues exist on a neutral field: the accent means now, moss means earned. Everything else — roughly 90% of any screen — is the neutral ramp below. This page is generated to match apex-tokens.css verbatim; if a screen disagrees with this page, the screen is wrong.

1. The model — two hues, everything else neutral · locked

Exactly two hues exist in APEX. Everything that isn't one of these two is neutral — text, borders, surfaces, fields. Tints are always 10%-alpha derivatives of their hue, generated, never hand-picked.

TokenHexMeaning
accent.primary #659BC8 Now — the current set, the selected control, the one primary CTA. The halo cast derives from it. Confirmed default (v8); see §4.
accent.primary-tint #659BC81A 10%-alpha derivative — chip fills, suggestion backgrounds behind accent content.
state.logged #6CA383 Earned — logged sets, completions, positive deltas. Kept deliberately distinct from accent so done-vs-now never shares a color.
state.logged-tint #6CA3831A 10%-alpha derivative — the logged suggestion bar, completion backgrounds.

If a third hue appears anywhere — a badge, an icon tint, a chart series, a block color — the design is wrong. Route the distinction through structure, size, or copy instead.

Negative and cautionary states never get a hue. A missed session, a validation error, an overdue deload — all render in neutral text plus copy: Missed is var(--text-2) on a neutral border, exactly like every other secondary label on the page — not red, not amber.

2. The neutral ramp · locked

~90% of any screen. Depth on the field comes from the halo and the card gradient (§3), not from color variety.

TokenHexRole
field-base #090A0C Screen background — bottom of the radial falloff.
field-mid #0C0E11 Radial midpoint (55% stop).
halo derived Accent-cast at whisper intensity; geometry ellipse 90% 45% at 50% −5%. Follows the accent parameter — blue → #141A22, violet → #161821. See §3–§4.
card-top #14161B Vertical card gradient — lit face.
card-base #0E1013 Vertical card gradient — foot.
border #20232A Hairline, 1px, all four card edges.
border-lt #2C3039 Top edge only — the machined highlight.
text #EDEEF0 Primary — numerals, card names, screen titles.
text-2 #8F9098 Secondary — prescriptions, captions.
text-muted #55575F Muted — set indices, disabled state.

3. The light — one source, told twice · locked

Depth comes from a single overhead light, told twice at half strength each: a faint radial halo behind the header lights the room; a shallow vertical card gradient with a brighter top edge lights the objects on it. Depth sums across the two — it never stacks into a third effect.

Card surface
Lit face → gradient foot

The vertical gradient plus the brighter border-lt top edge is the object's half of the light. The halo behind this panel is the room's half.

/* halo — sits behind the header, on the screen field */
background: radial-gradient(ellipse 90% 45% at 50% -5%,
  var(--halo) 0%, var(--field-mid) 55%, var(--field-base) 100%);

/* card — lit face to gradient foot, brighter top edge */
background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--card-top) 0%, var(--card-base) 100%);
border: 1px solid var(--border);
border-top-color: var(--border-lt);

4. The accent parameter · one-line token swap

Accent is a token, not a decision. Default is dusty blue #659BC8 (confirmed v8 after the challenge rounds); dusty violet #8A91C9 is retained as the alternate value. The halo derives from whichever accent is active — blue casts #141A22, violet casts #161821 — so swapping the parameter re-lights the whole screen, not just one control. Moss (earned) never moves.

Bench Press
4×6 @ 82.5kg · RPE 8
1 82.5kg × 6 ✓
2 82.5kg × 6 Now ●
3 82.5kg × 6 ·
Default — dusty blue
Bench Press
4×6 @ 82.5kg · RPE 8
1 82.5kg × 6 ✓
2 82.5kg × 6 Now ●
3 82.5kg × 6 ·
Alternate — dusty violet
/* the entire diff between the two cards above */
.variant-violet {
  --accent: var(--accent-alt);       /* #8A91C9 */
  --halo: var(--halo-alt);           /* #161821 */
  --accent-tint: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-alt) 10%, transparent);
}

Set 1 (done) and the neutral ramp are byte-identical between the two cards — only --accent, --accent-tint, and --halo move, scoped to one ancestor element. No component was touched, no new hex was introduced.

5. Provenance · locked

Every value on this page is generated, not authored here. The source of truth is tokens/apex.json in the cadre repo; npm run tokens emits generated/css/apex-tokens.css, whose :root block this page — and every screen under docs/design-system/templates/ and docs/design-system/screens/ — copies verbatim. Change a color by editing tokens/apex.json and regenerating; never by hand-editing a :root block downstream.

The retired indigo system is not deleted — it survives only as a historical record in context/current-state-inventory.html, flagged for retirement. It does not appear anywhere else in the design system, including this page.
